description: Docker daemon logs spurious 'ShouldRestart failed' warnings during graceful shutdown because handleContainerExit treats ErrRestartCanceled the same as genuine errors

root_cause.summary: When the daemon shuts down, it calls container.ExitOnNext() to stop the restart manager. When containerd sends the TaskDelete event, handleContainerExit calls RestartManager().ShouldRestart(), which returns ErrRestartCanceled. This error is logged as a WARN regardless of whether the daemon is shutting down. The 'ignoring event' message in libcontainerd/remote/client.go is also misleading.
root_cause.mechanism: handleContainerExit does not distinguish between ErrRestartCanceled (expected during shutdown) and other errors (genuine failures). Both are logged at WARN level.

error_chain.action: Calls container.ExitOnNext() to stop restart manager for each container
Container exits, shim sends TaskDelete event
Receives TaskDelete, logs confusing 'ignoring event' message
BUG: Calls ShouldRestart(), gets ErrRestartCanceled, logs as WARN

remediation: In handleContainerExit: check if the error from ShouldRestart is ErrRestartCanceled; if so, do not log it as a warning (it's expected during shutdown). In libcontainerd/remote/client.go: change 'ignoring event' to a more descriptive message like 'received task-delete event from containerd'. Also split exitStatus into separate exitCode and exitedAt fields for readability.
